title: Azure’s Lifesteal
description: Defeat your enemies and steal their hearts to grow stronger — but beware: every death may tip the scales. Rise by blood, or fall into oblivion.
body:
🌌 Lifesteal

Lifesteal adds a heart-stealing system to Minecraft.
Defeat other players to steal their maximum health, growing stronger with every victory. Lose hearts on death — survive at all costs or risk losing everything.

Strength is survival.

✨ Features:

➤ Steal Maximum Health:
Gain extra hearts by defeating other players.

➤ Lose Hearts on Death:
Dying reduces your total maximum health — stay alive to stay strong.

➤ Withdraw Hearts:
Use /trigger withdraw set <amount> to safely store or trade hearts with others.

➤ Multiplayer Ready:
Fully functional in multiplayer worlds — challenge your friends or form alliances.

➤ Resourcepack Visuals:
Includes custom visuals (requires the resourcepack) to track heart withdrawals.

➤ Lightweight and Flexible:
Simple to install, with no mods required — only a datapack and resourcepack.



⚙️ Setup Instructions:

Install both the Lifesteal Datapack and the Resourcepack into your world.
(The resourcepack is required for visuals and proper function.)

Run /reload after adding the datapack to initialize everything.

Make sure the resourcepack is active in your settings.


🩸 Withdrawing Hearts:
To remove hearts (for trading, storage, or gifting), use the command:
/trigger withdraw set <amount>
(Example: /trigger withdraw set 3 withdraws 3 hearts.)


❗ Important Notes:
This datapack requires the resourcepack
to work correctly.
Supports multiplayer
— heart stealing only works between players!
Designed for survival worlds, but can be adapted to other settings.

✍️ Credits:

Created by Azure (DeepAzureBlue).
Feedback and suggestions are welcome!
